title: rubus lasiodermis
intro: Rubus lasiodermis, also known as the thimbleberry or thimble-brush, is a species of bramble that belongs to the rose family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The thimbleberry can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in some modern herbal remedies.
